title: The Quiet Mathematics of Snowflakes
dek: A slow, contemplative look at how six-fold symmetry emerges from nothing but water and cold — and why no two are alike.

title: Why Everything Is Moving Faster Than You Think
dek: A propulsive argument that acceleration, not progress, is the defining texture of the present.

title: In Praise of the Three-Minute Pop Song
dek: A joyful, irreverent defense of brevity, hooks, and the art of saying everything in a verse and a chorus.

title: On the Difficulty of Living an Examined Life
dek: A grave meditation on self-knowledge, and why Socrates' instruction is harder than it sounds.

title: The Letters My Grandmother Never Sent
dek: An intimate, first-person essay on inheritance, silence, and the things we leave unwritten.

title: What Markets Get Wrong About Time
dek: A sweeping, systems-level argument about discounting, the future, and the price of patience.

title: The Forgotten Pigment That Changed Renaissance Blue
dek: A deep, specialist dive into ultramarine, lapis lazuli, and the chemistry of devotion.

title: A Brief History of Everyday Objects
dek: A wide-ranging, generalist tour through the hidden design of the things we never notice.

title: The Grief That Has No Name
dek: An emotionally resonant exploration of ambiguous loss and the feelings our language fails to hold.

title: The Engineering Behind a Perfectly Flat Table
dek: A cool, precise account of tolerance, flatness, and the unglamorous craft of making things level.

title: Cinema Without a Story
dek: An abstract essay on slow cinema, duration, and films that ask you to simply watch.

title: How a Beaver Builds a Dam, Step by Step
dek: A concrete, hands-on field account of engineering instinct, mud, and moving water.
